factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in airfreight and ocean freight services, driven by high average rates and strong customer demand.

evidence.text: Revenues and directly related operating expenses increased 72% and 81%, respectively, from strong growth in all services propelled by high average rates and growth in volumes transacted.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating with strong growth in all services, driven by high average rates and strong customer demand.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with significant increases in shipments from existing and new customers.

evidence.text: Revenues and directly related expenses increased 42% and 51%, respectively, in 2021, primarily due to an increase in shipments from existing and new customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog is growing significantly due to increased shipments from both existing and new customers.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in airfreight and ocean freight services, which experienced significant growth.

evidence.text: Airfreight services revenues and expenses increased 58% and 60%, respectively, in 2021, as compared with 2020, due to a 26% increase in tonnage and 28% and 29% increases in average sell and buy rates, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments with significant growth in airfreight and ocean freight services.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong demand and increased pricing.

evidence.text: Revenues and directly related operating expenses increased 72% and 81%, respectively, from strong growth in all services propelled by high average rates and growth in volumes transacted.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong demand and increased pricing.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are in North Asia and South Asia, particularly for airfreight and ocean freight services.

evidence.text: North Asia and South Asia ocean freight and ocean services revenues increased 213% and 295%, respectively, while directly related expenses increased 209% and 306%, respectively, primarily due to higher average sell and buy rates and increases in containers shipped.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in end markets, particularly in North Asia and South Asia, indicates positive momentum.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are not explicitly mentioned, but the company is managing costs through efficient buying and operational adjustments.

evidence.text: By consolidating shipments from multiple customers and concentrating our buying power, we are able to negotiate favorable buy rates from the direct carriers, while at the same time offering lower sell rates than customers would otherwise be able to negotiate themselves.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is effectively managing costs through efficient buying and operational adjustments.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to operational efficiencies and the ability to negotiate favorable buy rates through consolidated shipments.

evidence.text: By consolidating shipments from multiple customers and concentrating our buying power, we are able to negotiate favorable buy rates from the direct carriers, while at the same time offering lower sell rates than customers would otherwise be able to negotiate themselves.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are evident through operational efficiencies and favorable buy rates.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety incidents are reported in the text.

evidence.text: The text does not mention any product quality, safety incidents, or warranty costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No significant quality or safety incidents are reported, indicating stable performance in this area.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered forward guidance, but expects continued challenges due to supply chain disruptions and the cyber-attack.

evidence.text: The Company expects that the impact of the shutdown and the ongoing impacts of the cyber-attack will have a material adverse impact on its business, revenues, expenses, results of operations, cash flows and reputation. At this early stage, the Company is unable to estimate the ultimate direct and indirect financial impacts of this cyber-attack.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Management anticipates significant adverse impacts from supply chain issues and a cyber-attack, indicating clear deterioration.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to ongoing supply chain disruptions, cyber-attack impacts, and potential risks from inflation and trade policies.

evidence.text: The Company expects that the impact of the shutdown and the ongoing impacts of the cyber-attack will have a material adverse impact on its business, revenues, expenses, results of operations, cash flows and reputation. At this early stage, the Company is unable to estimate the ultimate direct and indirect financial impacts of this cyber-attack.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution due to multiple risks including cyber-attack impacts and inflation, showing low confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain disruptions, cyber-attack impacts, and potential challenges in managing labor and transportation costs.

evidence.text: The Company expects that the impact of the shutdown and the ongoing impacts of the cyber-attack will have a material adverse impact on its business, revenues, expenses, results of operations, cash flows and reputation. At this early stage, the Company is unable to estimate the ultimate direct and indirect financial impacts of this cyber-attack.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks are severe, including supply chain and cyber-attack impacts, which could significantly affect operations.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on maintaining a strong global logistics network and investing in technology to enhance efficiency.

evidence.text: We will continue to make important investments in people, processes and technology, as well as to invest in our strategic efforts to explore new areas for profitable growth. In the first quarter of 2022, we are incurring incremental expenses relating to investigating and remediating the cyber-attack and expect to continue to incur such expenses, which will significantly increase our overhead expenses.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is investing in logistics and technology, but the focus is on maintaining existing strategies without clear positive outlook.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, currency fluctuations, and labor shortages, which could impact transportation costs and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Because fuel is an integral part of carriers' costs and impacts both our buy rates and sell rates, we would expect our revenues and costs to be impacted as carriers adjust rates for the effect of changing fuel prices. To the extent that future fuel prices increase and we are unable to pass through the increase to our customers, fuel price increases could adversely affect our operating income.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to fuel prices and currency fluctuations pose moderate risks to transportation costs and efficiency.

factors.summary: A potential catalyst is the resolution of the cyber-attack and related expenses, which could impact future operations and financial results.

evidence.text: The Company expects that the impact of the shutdown and the ongoing impacts of the cyber-attack will have a material adverse impact on its business, revenues, expenses, results of operations, cash flows and reputation. At this early stage, the Company is unable to estimate the ultimate direct and indirect financial impacts of this cyber-attack.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The resolution of the cyber-attack could be a catalyst, but current impacts are adverse, indicating a negative outlook.

factors.summary: Global economic conditions are facing challenges due to the COVID-19 pandemic and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: The global economy entered a recession as a result of the pandemic, which initially affected trade and negatively affected demand for our services for a period of time, before rebounding in 2021. Future unfavorable economic conditions and high inflation could result in lower freight volumes and adversely affect Expeditors' revenues, operating results and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Global economic uncertainty impacted trade and could affect demand for our services or the financial stability of our service providers and customers.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic challenges from the pandemic and inflation are expected to negatively impact revenues and cash flows.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors, such as trade restrictions and conflicts, pose risks to the business.

evidence.text: Changes in labor and other costs, including the potential impacts of inflation; increased global concerns regarding working conditions and environmental sustainability; changes in consumer attitudes regarding goods made in countries other than their own; changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical risks like trade restrictions pose moderate threats to the business.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations and sustainability initiatives could increase costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: The increasing concern over climate change has resulted and may continue to result in more regulations relating to climate change, including regulating greenhouse gas emissions, restrictions on modes of transportation, alternative energy policies and sustainability initiatives, such as the FuelEU Maritime initiative.
evidence.subsection: Climate change, including measures to address climate change, could adversely impact our business and financial results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased costs and operational constraints due to environmental regulations pose a risk.

factors.summary: Trade tariffs and restrictions could negatively impact the business.

evidence.text: Changes in and application of international and domestic customs, trade and security regulations; wars, strikes, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts; changes in labor and other costs, including the potential impacts of inflation; increased global concerns regarding working conditions and environmental sustainability; changes in consumer attitudes regarding goods made in countries other than their own; changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Trade tariffs and restrictions could negatively impact the business.

factors.summary: Interest rate fluctuations could impact the global economy and, in turn, affect the business.

evidence.text: Interest rate fluctuations; changes in and application of international and domestic customs, trade and security regulations; wars, strikes, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts; changes in labor and other costs, including the potential impacts of inflation; increased global concerns regarding working conditions and environmental sustainability; changes in consumer attitudes regarding goods made in countries other than their own; changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fluctuations could negatively impact the global economy and the business.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rate fluctuations could impact the business.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rates and currency control regulations; interest rate fluctuations; changes in and application of international and domestic customs, trade and security regulations; wars, strikes, civil unrest, acts of terrorism, and other conflicts; changes in labor and other costs, including the potential impacts of inflation; increased global concerns regarding working conditions and environmental sustainability; changes in consumer attitudes regarding goods made in countries other than their own; changes in the price and readily available quantities of oil and other petroleum-related products.
evidence.subsection: Any reduction in international commerce or disruption in global trade may adversely impact our business and operating results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Currency fluctuations pose a risk to the business.

factors.summary: Talent retention is a concern due to the shift to remote work and potential turnover.

evidence.text: As the pandemic restrictions ease, we are requiring employees to return to the office. As a result, for those individuals that prefer working remotely, we may experience a higher degree of turnover and lower employee satisfaction in the near future. Further, this could inhibit our ability to identify, recruit, and hire new employees.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential higher turnover due to office return policies.

factors.summary: Adoption of digital technologies is crucial for maintaining competitiveness.

evidence.text: Expeditors relies heavily and must compete based upon the flexibility and sophistication of the technologies utilized in performing our core businesses. Development and maintenance of these systems must be accomplished in a cost-effective manner and support the use of secure protocols, including integration and availability of third-party technology. We are continually enhancing our systems, including significant upgrades to core operating and accounting systems.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Adoption of digital technologies supports competitiveness.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, including those related to security and environmental policies, could increase costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: Expeditors is affected by ever increasing regulations from a number of sources in the United States and in foreign locations in which we operate. Many of these regulations are complex and require varying degrees of interpretation, including those related to handling dangerous and hazardous materials, trade compliance, data privacy, environmental, employment, compensation and competition, and may result in unforeseen costs.
evidence.subsection: We are subject to a complex regulatory environment, and failure to comply with and adapt to these regulations could result in penalties or otherwise adversely impact our business.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes could increase costs and constraints.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a risk as many rely on multiple logistics providers.

evidence.text: Many larger customers utilize the services of multiple logistics providers. Customers regularly solicit bids from competitors in order to improve service and to secure favorable pricing and contractual terms such as longer payment terms, fixed-price arrangements, higher or unlimited liability limits and performance penalties.
evidence.subsection: Our industry is highly competitive, and failure to compete or respond to customer requirements could damage our business and results of operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Reliance on multiple providers may lead to competitive pressures.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is necessary due to the shift in work environments and remote work preferences.

evidence.text: As the pandemic restrictions ease, we are requiring employees to return to the office. As a result, for those individuals that prefer working remotely, we may experience a higher degree of turnover and lower employee satisfaction in the near future. Further, this could inhibit our ability to identify, recruit, and hire new employees.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company anticipates higher turnover and lower satisfaction due to mandatory office returns, which could hinder recruitment.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising significantly, driven by supply chain disruptions, inflationary pressures, and challenges in securing sufficient transportation capacity. The company faces longer transportation times and difficulties in meeting growing customer demand due to congestion at key gateways.

evidence.text: Significant congestion at ocean ports and airport gateways from continued shortages in equipment, labor and warehousing space at destinations and disruptions from COVID-19 precautionary measures, resulted in longer transportation times and created challenging conditions to meet the growing customer demand.

Many of our customers are experiencing disruptions in their revenues and cash flows and have incurred higher operating expenses as a result of supply chain cost increases and other inflationary pressures.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising costs due to supply chain issues, inflation, and transportation problems are causing significant financial strain.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed outcomes in its pricing power. While it has successfully increased average sell and buy rates across all services, it faces challenges in passing on rate increases to customers due to volatile market conditions, which can negatively impact margins.

evidence.text: Average sell rates and buy rates increased in all regions, with the most significant increases on exports out of North Asia and South Asia.

Volatile market conditions can create situations where rate increases charged by carriers and other service providers are implemented with little or no advance notice. We often cannot pass these rate increases on to our customers in the same time frame, if at all. As a result, our yields and margins can be negatively impacted, as recently experienced.
evidence.subsection: Difficulty in forecasting timing or volumes of customer shipments or rate changes by carriers could adversely impact our margins and operating results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: While pricing has increased, inability to pass on rate hikes to customers threatens margins.

factors.summary: The company is observing mixed trends in volume_trends, with growth in unit volumes across all services, particularly in airfreight and ocean freight, but also facing volatility due to capacity constraints and market conditions, which could lead to fluctuating rates and potential improvements as Covid-19 disruptions ease.

evidence.text: Tonnage increased in all regions, with the largest increase coming from exports out of North America and North Asia.

Throughout 2021 and into 2022, transportation rates have risen to historically high levels. We believe that as Covid-19 disruptions recede, and passenger air travel rebounds, that port congestion will improve. This will result in additional transportation capacity becoming available. Rates will likely decline as this occurs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed trends with growth but also volatility and capacity issues; outlook depends on pandemic recovery.

factors.summary: Expeditors faces risks related to potential material litigation or investigations that could impact its financial results. Additionally, a recent cyber-attack has increased vulnerability to legal claims, including breach of contract claims, which could further affect the company's financial position and reputation.

evidence.text: As a multinational corporation, Expeditors is subject to formal or informal investigations from governmental authorities or others in the countries in which we do business. In addition, we may become subject to civil litigation with our customers, service providers and other parties with whom we do business. These investigations and litigation may require significant management time and could cause us to incur substantial additional legal and related costs, which may include fines, penalties or damages that could have a materially adverse impact on our financial results.
evidence.subsection: Investigations and litigation could require management time and or incur substantial legal costs or fines, penalties or damages, any of which could adversely impact on our financial results.

evidence.text: This cyber-attack could also result in increased vulnerability to attempts of fraud, legal claims and proceedings including potential breach of contract claims, reporting delays or errors; interference with regulatory reporting; an increase in costs to protect our systems and technology; or damage to our reputation.
evidence.subsection: Any significant disruptions to our network and systems continuity could have an adverse impact to our business and financial results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential legal risks and cyber-attack vulnerabilities pose significant threats to financial and reputational health.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance, with expansion in gross and operating margins driven by strong revenue growth and increased pricing, yet facing challenges such as pricing volatility and increased costs that are putting pressure on profitability.

evidence.text: Net earnings to shareholders increased 103% and we returned $710 million to shareholders in common stock repurchases and dividends.

These freight market conditions have created and continue to create pricing volatility that challenges Expeditors’ ability to maintain historical unitary profitability. Many of our customers are experiencing disruptions in their revenues and cash flows and have incurred higher operating expenses as a result of supply chain cost increases and other inflationary pressures.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margin expansion is happening, but pricing volatility and cost increases are pressuring profitability.

factors.summary: Labor costs are a significant expense and are creating margin pressure. The company is facing challenges in managing these costs due to increases in wages, headcount, and operational demands. Additionally, remote work and potential turnover further complicate cost management.

evidence.text: Salaries and related expenses increased 34% in 2021, as compared with 2020, principally due to increases in commissions and bonuses earned from higher revenues and operating income and a 9% increase in headcount to support the growth in our operations.

We may incur higher compensation-related expense to recruit and retain employees. We believe that our compensation programs are among the unique characteristics responsible for differentiating our performance from that of many of our competitors.
evidence.subsection: We are dependent on our personnel and any inability to hire, develop or retain our key employees may have a negative impact on our operations.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor expenses due to wage increases and headcount growth are impacting margins.

factors.summary: The supply chain continues to face significant disruptions, primarily due to port congestion, labor shortages, and insufficient warehousing space. These issues are exacerbated by ongoing challenges from COVID-19 precautionary measures, leading to longer transportation times and difficulties in meeting customer demand.

evidence.text: Significant congestion at ocean ports and airport gateways from continued shortages in equipment, labor and warehousing space at destinations and disruptions from COVID-19 precautionary measures, resulted in longer transportation times and created challenging conditions to find availability to meet the growing customer demand.

Congestion at ports resulting from labor and equipment shortages and insufficient warehousing space at destinations. Air freighters and charters, container ships and gateway infrastructure are operating at near maximum capacity.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Severe disruptions from port congestion, labor shortages, and COVID-19 measures are causing major operational issues.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is high, particularly in airfreight and ocean freight services, with carriers operating at near maximum capacity. However, capacity utilization is constrained due to pandemic-related disruptions and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Freighters, charters and gateway infrastructure are operating at near maximum capacity, which is continuing the pressure on buy rates and limiting the ability to move additional volume.

Air freighters and charters, container ships and gateway infrastructure are operating at near maximum capacity. While we believe these constraints are not long-term in nature, they impact our current ability to move increased air and ocean volumes in these capacity-constrained regions.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: High capacity utilization with pandemic-related constraints indicates significant operational stress and risks.

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in managing inventory levels due to supply chain disruptions, including congestion at transportation hubs and labor shortages, which may lead to shortages and longer transportation times.

evidence.text: Significant congestion at ocean ports and airport gateways from continued shortages in equipment, labor and warehousing space at destinations and disruptions from COVID-19 precautionary measures, resulted in longer transportation times and created challenging conditions to meet growing customer demand.

Our facilities and employees are operating under the constraints of special protective measures and many have been working remotely. As discussed in more detail under 'Results of Operations', there are significant constraints on current capacity for both air freight and ocean freight.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Inventory management challenges due to supply chain disruptions and labor shortages pose major risks.
